headline: Bayeux Tapestry arrives in London for forthcoming display at British Museum
description: The thousand-year-old, 70-meter-long embroidery depicting William the Conqueror's exploits in medieval England has been removed from its crate after its transfer from France. Staff will begin a thorough check of its condition on Monday, and it will be placed in a custom-made display case a few days later.

Bombing intensifies as US strikes Iran for the seventh night
description: Iran reported at least three dead and retaliated with attacks on several US military sites in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain.

headline: Elie Barnavi: 'Marwan Barghouti is the only one capable of uniting the Palestinian people around a national project and the two-state solution'
description: The former Israeli ambassador to France, in an op-ed for Le Monde, calls for the release of the Palestinian politician, who has been held in Israel since 2002. He argues that Barghouti has consistently championed the project of a sovereign Palestine living in peace alongside Israel.

headline: Argentina's thriving theater scene
description: Buenos Aires has emerged as the epicenter of Spanish-language theater, where a vibrant creative scene – both precarious and remarkably prolific – is defined by the generosity of its performances.

headline: Christophe B., an ordinary man accused of extraordinary violence in 'libertine' rapes
description: The 56-year-old is accused of forcing his partners to engage in extreme sexual acts with men he recruited online for over 20 years. He is charged with rapes with torture or acts of barbarity.
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

description: A man identified as Christophe B. is accused of having forced six former partners into extremely violent sexual encounters with dozens of men recruited online over a period of more than 20 years, under the guise of 'libertinism.'
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

headline: Who are the people setting France's forests ablaze?
description: Nearly nine out of 10 forest fires in France are started by humans. Some do so deliberately, with the perpetrators often difficult to identify.

headline: Andy Burnham, Britain's next prime minister, puts social justice at top of agenda
description: The Makerfield MP has been declared Labour leader and is set to succeed Keir Starmer on Monday. His platform remains vague and he will have to contend with tight public finances.
